message:
Running command: # The issue is the /nc/apps/bin/ipm wrapper is broken (literal \n in shebang)
# The ipm_linker calls subprocess.run(["ipm", ...]) which hits the broken wrapper
# Solution: patch ipm_linker to use the real binary path, or fix via env PATH trick
# Best solution: set IPM env var or pass full path via a local shim

# Create a proper working shim that subprocess can use
cat > /usr/local/bin/ipm << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
PYTHONPATH=/nc/apps/ipm exec /nc/apps/ipm/bin/ipm "$@"
EOF
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ipm

# Verify subprocess can call it
python3 -c "
import subprocess, shutil
print('shutil.which:', shutil.which('ipm'))
res = subprocess.run(['/usr/local/bin/ipm', '--version'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print('direct call:', res.returncode, res.stdout.strip())
res2 = subprocess.run(['ipm', '--version'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print('PATH call:', res2.returncode, res2.stdout.strip(), res2.stderr.strip())
"
